_meta.description: Agent-side irritation patterns — phrases and structures in LLM output that empirically correlate with user frustration. Mirror of the user-side Grievance Dictionary.
sources: Sharma et al. (2023) — Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models (Anthropic)
Brendel et al. (2023) — The paradoxical role of humanness in aggression toward conversational agents (JMIS)
Crolic et al. (2022) — Blame the Bot: Anthropomorphism and Anger in Customer-Chatbot Interactions
Ozuem et al. (2024) — Chatbots, service failure recovery and customer loyalty (Psych & Marketing)
ITP (2024) — When chatbots fail: exploring user coping (Emerald)
Weiler et al. (2023) — Seeking empathy or suggesting a solution? (Electronic Markets)
Pavone et al. (2023) — Rage against the machine (J. Interactive Marketing)
_meta.usage: Match against agent draft output. If patterns fire, inject constraint or promote to dynamic ban.

sycophancy.description: False validation — agreeing with the user to please rather than to be accurate. Reinforced by RLHF.

nl: dat is een hele goede vraag
daar heb je helemaal gelijk in
je hebt een heel goed punt

fake_humanity.description: Performative empathy — simulating emotional understanding without substantive engagement. Identified as distinct failure mode by ITP (2024).

nl: ik begrijp hoe je je voelt
het is logisch dat je je zo voelt
dank je wel dat je dit deelt
wat fijn dat je dit met me deelt
je staat er niet alleen voor
en: I'm sorry you're going through this
I can imagine how that feels
it makes sense that you feel that way
thank you for sharing this with me
I can see why that would be frustrating

helpdesk_filler.description: Empty responsiveness — offering help structurally without substance. Generic service-desk language that signals the agent isn't actually engaging.

nl: kan ik je nog ergens mee helpen
ik ga mijn best doen om
en: shall I do that for you
is there anything else I can help with

overexplanation.description: Paternalistic over-elaboration — explaining things the user already knows, bullet-pointing emotional topics, unsolicited pedagogical framing.

nl: het is belangrijk om te begrijpen dat
om het even samen te vatten
en: the key thing to understand is

incorrect_repair.description: Acknowledging a correction without actually changing behaviour. The agent says 'I understand' but repeats the same error or pattern. Identified by Pavone et al. (2023).

nl: ik begrijp het, ik zal het anders doen
en: I understand, I'll do it differently

incorrect_repair.note: These patterns alone are not irritating — they become irritating when followed by repetition of the original error. Detection requires pairing with repetition-detection module.

emotional_incongruence.description: Wrong register at the wrong moment — cheerful response to anger, enthusiasm about distress, exclamation marks during serious topics. Crolic et al. (2022) showed this amplifies negative affect.
emotional_incongruence.note: Context-dependent: these are only irritating when the user's emotional state is negative (level 1+). At level 0, they are appropriate.

premature_solutioning.description: Jumping to fixes before the user has been heard. Weiler et al. (2023) showed solution-oriented messages score on competence but not warmth — when the user needs warmth first, solutioning is friction.

nl: hier is wat je kunt doen

premature_solutioning.note: Context-dependent: only friction when user expressed emotion without asking for advice.
